name: Faith Cooperative Federal Credit Union
description_short: Faith Cooperative FCU is a Dallas-based, NCUA-insured credit union founded in 1959, offering savings accounts, CDs, auto loans, personal loans, and mortgages.
description_long: Faith Cooperative Federal Credit Union traces its roots to 1959, when it was established as St. John Federal Credit Union under the leadership of Dr. E. C. Estell, a pastor and civil rights activist in Dallas, Texas. The institution describes itself as 'heir to a legacy of a declaration of economic independence,' reflecting a founding mission to deliver financial access to an underserved community. Today it operates from a single administrative office on West Wheatland Road in South Dallas, continuing that mission under its current name. FCFCU offers a focused range of financial products typical of a community credit union. On the deposit side, members can open a Basic Savings Account, a Club Account for goal-based saving, or Certificates of Deposit. For borrowing, the credit union provides Auto Loans, Personal Loans, and Mortgage Loans, along with Gap Protection for vehicle financing. An ACH form is available for electronic payment setup, and current rates are published on the website for member transparency. As a federally chartered credit union, FCFCU is insured by the NCUA up to $250,000 per depositor, backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. government — the same protection level as FDIC-insured banks. Its published routing number (311080285) confirms it functions as a fully operational depository institution. Being member-owned means any surplus is returned to members rather than outside shareholders, which typically translates to more competitive loan rates and lower fees than commercial banks. Its civil rights heritage gives it a community-first identity that distinguishes it from transactional lenders. Faith Cooperative FCU is a small, single-branch operation with significantly restricted hours — Monday through Thursday, 9:00 am to 3:00 pm only, excluding holidays. This is a real accessibility constraint for working adults. The website offers minimal detail on membership eligibility criteria, digital banking features, or online account access. Prospective members should call (972) 228-5222 directly to confirm qualification requirements, fee schedules, and whether remote banking options are available before applying.
pros: NCUA-insured up to $250,000 — full federal deposit protection
Founded in 1959 — over 65 years of continuous operation
Civil rights heritage rooted in economic empowerment for underserved Dallas communities
Offers a broad loan suite: auto, personal, and mortgage loans under one roof
Gap Protection available for auto loan borrowers
Certificates of Deposit available for members seeking fixed-rate savings
Published routing number (311080285) confirms full-service depository status
cons: Extremely limited hours: Monday–Thursday 9:00 am–3:00 pm only, closed Friday through Sunday
Single physical branch — no network of locations in the Dallas area
Website does not disclose membership eligibility requirements
No mention of online banking, mobile app, or remote deposit features
Minimal transparency on fee schedules despite a fees page link
best_for: Dallas-area residents who qualify for membership and can visit during weekday business hours
Borrowers seeking community-based auto or personal loans from a member-owned institution
Savers looking for federally insured CDs or a basic savings account with lower fees
Individuals who value credit unions with a historic civil rights and economic empowerment mission
diagnosis: Faith Cooperative FCU is best suited for Dallas residents who meet membership eligibility and can visit in person Monday through Thursday before 3:00 pm. The main caveat is its very restricted hours and single-branch footprint, which makes it impractical as a primary institution for anyone reliant on evening, weekend, or digital banking access.
services: Secure email contact and web contact form
